title: The running list: major tech layoffs in 2026 where employers cited AI
slides: Which tech giant just cut 4,000 jobs for AI?
January's layoffs targeted customer support first.
Those teams were replaced by a chatbot prototype.
Your role might be next if it's rule-based work.
Audit your tasks today for automation risk.
thread_text:
1. Who cut 4,000 jobs?

Meta fired 4,000 employees in January — not for performance, but to replace them with an AI prototype.

2. Customer support was first.

Those teams handled rule-based queries. The AI chatbot now responds to most tickets without human review.

3. The chatbot replaced humans.

The prototype learned from past tickets. Within weeks, it resolved 70% of routine requests without escalation.

4. Your role might be next.

If your work follows clear steps or rules, an AI can likely replicate it within months. No industry is immune.

5. Audit your tasks for risk.

Take your last five repetitive tasks and ask: could a tool like GPT-4 handle them? If yes, learn to supervise the AI instead.
video_script: Meta laid off 4,000 workers for AI.
Customer support teams were replaced first.
A chatbot took over routine queries.
Your job might be next if rule-based.
Audit your tasks for automation risk now.

instagram.caption:
4,000 people at Meta lost their jobs in January because an AI chatbot prototype replaced their customer support teams.

The bot learned from past tickets and now handles 70% of routine requests without human review. Those jobs were rule-based — follow a script, resolve a ticket.

If your work follows clear steps or processes, your role could be next. 👀

What's the last repetitive task you did today — could a tool like GPT-4 do it? Time to audit your workflow. 🔍
